source_text: WALCOT, Desmond Bielby.—b. 1918 ; ed. Boys' Gram. Sch., Suva and Mt. Albert Gram. Sch., Auckland, N.Z. ; cik., Fiji, 1936 ; clk. and customs offr., gr. II, G. and E. Is. Col., 1940 ; gr. I., 1946.

FOLLENFANT, H. G., O.B.E. (1945), T.D. (1947).—b. 1908 ; ed. Raines Sch. and Lond. Univ. ; mil. serv., 1939–45, col. ; dep. dir., transpn., B.A.O.R., 1945 ; dist. engnr., P.W.D., Ken., 1948 ; res. engnr., 1949 ; div. engnr., 1950 ; gen. man., rlwys., N. Borneo, 1953.
KRETSCHMAR, J. M.—Acting chief clerk and draughtsman, surveyor-general's office; second class clerk, treasury, Natal, June, 1881; clerk assistant and librarian legislative council, 1883.

flagged_value.position: clerk assistant and librarian legislative council

source_text: ISHER, V. M.—Medical offr., E.A.P., Mar., 9.

EWELL, NORMAN PARSONS.—M.C. (1917); D.; B.Ch.; B.A.O., Trin. Coll., Dub.; L.M., Bunda, Dub.; B.A. (Moderator), Trin. Coll., D.; late anaesthetist, Dun's Hosp., Dub.; asst. offr., Seychelles, 1910; J.P., and chmn. bds. of health, Praslin and La Digue; med. t. of leper asylum; med. offr., E.A.P., 5.

ITZGERALD, BRYAN.—B. 1878; 3rd clerical t., chief commissary's off., B. Guiana, Aug., 3; 6th cl. clk., audit dept., May, 1895; 5th clk., Nov., 1896; ag. clk. of ct. and gold offr., st. of mines, Bartica, and navigation offr., for or Essequibo and its tributaries, Nov., 1900, to b., 1901, and May to July, 1901; clk of ct., entyne Coast judicial dist., Nov., 1901; J.P., st., 1902; 4th cls. clk., G.P.O., Feb., 1904; clk. st., Berbice judl. dist., Apr., 1904; J.P., 1907; t. postmr., G. Coast, Dec., 1907; 2nd cls. super- or, cust., 11th Mar., 1909; 1st cls. supervisor, t., 30th Nov., 1911; senr. collr., 1st Jan., 1.

AULOCK, HON. SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (02), K.C., LL.D., M.A.—B. 1843; graduated Toronto Univ., 1863; gold medal in modern languages; called to the Ontario bar, 1868; chanc. of Toronto Univ. in 1881; sat in lmt. 1882 to 1905, representing N. York; tmr.-gen. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 6; ch. just., exchequer div., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1905; ch. just., Ontario, 1923.
DEFORD, JOHN HOPE, B.A., M.D., B. Ch., L.O. (R.U.I.), L.M. (Dub.), D.T.M. (Liver- pool).—Civ. surg., South African Field Force, 1-2; med. offr., Uganda Prot., 1st Aug., 7.
STEELE, L. F.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Sept., 14 to Mar., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Feb., 20, apptd. after compet. exam., cler., offr., S.O., July., 1922.

URISON, SIR JAMES WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (19), B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1872; ed. privately at Trin. Hall, Camb.; snr. classical scholar, 1; 1st div., 2nd cls. class. tripos, 1894; cls. law tripos, Part II., 1895; Latham English prize, 1896; B.A., LL.B., 1895; called to the Bar, Middle Temple, Apr., 1896; devilled practised at the Chancery Bar, 1896-1902; isr., E.A.P., 9th Oct., 1902; town mag., Mbasa, Jan., 1904; 2nd asst. judge, H.B.M. rt for Zanzibar, Apr., 1904; sen. asst. judge, r., 1905; chief judge, Zanzibar, 20th Sept., 4; mem. of court of appeal for Eastern Africa, 1905-1910, and from 1914 upon transfer of Zanzibar to C.O.; judge of sup. ct. of H.H. tan of Zanzibar from 1908; arbitrator in dispute between Zanzibar and E.A.P. govt., as H.H. mainland properties, 1911; recd. thanks S. of S. for conversion of Applied Indian Acts, Zanzibar Decrees, 1917; judge of the Prize Ct., Zanzibar, from 1914; passed govt. exam. Hindustani, 1903, and in Swahili, 1904; miner in Swahili to the Zanzibar Govt.; attorney-gen., S. Stmts., 1919; ag. col. sec., e, 1920 to May, 1921; author of "Swahili Translations," 1911.

packets.reason: award 'Knight Bachelor' year not in source

source_text: ARR, CECIL WILLIAM CHASE, C.M.G. (1923), K.E.—Jun. offr., Perak civ. ser., 1889; dist. offr., Tampin, Negri Sembilan, 1897; dist. offr., Ng, 1904; ag. comsnr. of trade and customs, L.S., 1909; ag. British res., Negri Sembilan, 1; 2nd lieut. M.S.V.R., 1911; under sec., L.S., 1912; lieut. M.S.V.R., 1912; ag. agt., Information agency, London, 1912; seconded gov. of B. N. Borneo, Apr., 1913; lieut. mount offr.), 30th June to 7th Dec., 1915; fish res., Pahang, 5th Feb., 1916; capt., V.R., 10th Mar., 1916; mem., joint exam. S. Sttlmts. and F.M.S. for Malay, Apr., '; major, M.V.I., 1st Feb., 1920; Br. res., ck, 14th July, 1921; lieut. col., M.V.I., ck, Apr., 1923.

SHER, WALTER WHITBREAD.—B. 1886; at Marist Bros. Coll., Uitenhage; clk., Ziland, 1910; registr. and master, sp. ct. of Ziland, and Sheriff, 1916; dep. asst. comsr., Bane, 1919; served in Anglo-Boer War, S.W. campaign, 1914-15 and Flanders, -18.

HORNTON, GEORGE. — M.D. (Edin.); R.C.P.(Lond.); D.P.H.(Oxford); house physcn., in Royal Infirm., 1890; house surg., Metropolitan hosp., Lond.; house surg., children's p., Hull; asst. med. offr., Tooting Fever p., 1893-99; civil surg. to H.M. forces in Africa; med. supt., Pretoria hosp., 1900-8; med. supt., gen. hosp., Colombo, Ceylon, v., 1908; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Sept.-Dec., 9; asst. civ. med. offr., Jan., 1920; ag. prin. med. offr., May, 1922; ag. registr., Ceylon d. Coll., Jan. to Oct., 1923; ag. prin. civ. d. offr., Oct., 1924.
CARTER, THE HON. (1878).—Barrister, N. mem. house of 1861 to 1865; 1870, and f to Quebec, 1 the Domin for the admi nion; and rights of the ch. justice, 187 Sept., 1878, and the absence of and 1898.

flagged_value.position: and rights of the chief justice

source_text: VALTER, ROBERT, C.M.G. (1918), B.A.—B. 3; ed. at Marlborough, and Worc. Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. honours class. mods.; 3rd cls. honours lit. hum.; cadet F.M.S., Nov., 1896; sed in Chinese (Cant.), Apr., 1900; ag. asst. insnr. of pol., Perak, 1901; passed F.M.S. law m., 1901; seconded to Weihaiwei, Dec., 1901; to govt. and mag., Weihaiwei, Apr., 1902; seconded for ser. under Transvaal govt. as emigrn. , May, 1904; passed in Pekingese, May, 1904; returned to Weihaiwei as sec. to govt. and , Jan., 1906; ag. comsnr., Weihaiwei, June , 1909, and Sept.-Oct., 1913; passed bar exam., 1913; col. sec., B. Honduras, Feb., 4; admstd. govt., Mar., 1917 to Apr., 1918, from Aug., 1918 to Mar., 1919; war service recognised by S. of S., Nov., 1918; admstr., minica, May, 1920; admstr., St. Vincent, r., 1923.

IBBITS, ARTHUR CHARLES KENT. — B. 1877; sloyed col. sec.'s off.; treas. and aud. off., igua, Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. sav. bank, Jan., 5; 1st clk., post off., Jan., 1898; ag. postmr. 1899, 1900, 1901, 1903, 1904, 1905, 1906 and 1907; postmr. of Antigua, and federal postmr. of Leeward Is., 1st Apr., 1907; apptd. a library trustee and a J.P., Antigua, 1910; income and trade tax assessor in 1908, 1913, 1914 and 1915; chmn., bd. of guardians in 1911, 1913 and 1915; dir., P.O.G. fund, May, 1912; ag. asst. censor and ch. censor during war; dep. chmn., city comsnsr., 4th Dec., 1916; ag. offr. in ch., Antigua—Montserrat flashlight serv., Aug., 1918 to Feb., 1919.
BRANCH. J. R. A.—B. 1894; ed. Antigua Grammar Sch. and McGill Univ.; served in great war (3 meds.); sub-inspr., Leeward Is. pol., 1927-30; inspr., pol. 1931.

CAMPBELL-IRONS, A.—Ed. at Edin. Acad. and Univ. of Edin.; studied law and served in a lawyer's office; served in S. Africa, and recd. comsn. in A.S.C.; res. comsn. and joined reserve of offrs., A.S.C.; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 16th June, 1906. CAMPION, LIEUT.-COL. SIR WILLIAM ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1924), D.S.O.

RRETT, ARTHUR LUTLEY.—B. 1882; ent. affra. dept., Cape, Feb., 1901; clk., native ct., sec. to several deptl. comtees, and as sec. to Sir Walter Stanford; prot., natives arley, 1913; prin. clk., ch. native comsnr., 1920; mag., Ngamakwe, 1925; ch. clk., of ch. mag., Umtata and sec. and treas., keian Territories gen. coun., 1928; ch. clk., native affra., Pretoria, 1929; seconded as r. of conciliations, 1930; dir., native lab. n) and ch. native comsnr., Witwatersrand,

COOPER, PHILLIP WARD.—B. 1877; ed. Marlborough Coll.; col. audit branch, E. and A. Dept., 1st Dec., 1899; asst. local auditor, E. Africa and Uganda Protas., 9th Oct., 1900; ag. local auditor, Uganda, 23rd Dec., 1901 to 15th June, 1902; asst. collr., Uganda, 4th Jan., 1904, with seniority from 31st Mar., 1902; collr., 1st April, 1905; ag. prov. comsnr., Western Prov., Uganda, 9th Sept., 1913.

DISCOMBE, JOHN. —Apptd., after compet. exam., 3rd class clk., sup. ct., Gibraltar, 1892; 2nd class clk., 1899; asst. regisr. and dep. clk. of arraigns, 1900; called to the bar, Gray's Inn; regisr. and clk. of arraigns, admstr.-gen., ex officio J.P., and mem. of cemetery comtee., 1911; marshal, ex officio , 1913; regisr. and marshal, prize court, 1914; custodian of enemy property, 1916; during the war rendered legal assistance to the atty.-gen., in addition to other duties, July, 1916, to Feb., 1919; regisr. under Deeds of Arrangement Ordce., 1917; regisr. of business names, 1918; contrlr. local clearing office for enemy debts, 1920; ag. pol. mag. and coroner on several occasions.

GEOFFREY HERBERT, R.N. O.B.E. (Mil.).—B. 1881; served in R. 395-1926; China med., 1900; ment. in or services at Battle of Jutland when H.M.S. "Valiant"; awarded O.B.E., or services afloat during the Great War; carbmr., Plymouth, from 1919; elec. a brother, Trinity House Corpn., 1921; at admy., 1922; master attendant, S. , Apr., 1926.
GIBB, JOHN.—B. 1881; supervisor of warehouses, govt. stores, Cey'on, Oct., 1914; col. storekeeper, Sept., 1916.

L, CAPT. LEONARD JOSEPH, O.B.E., R.N.R.—B. 1879; cadet, H.M.S. "Wor- ", 1894-96; merchant service, 1896-1904; 1904-07; extra masters' B.O.T. cert.; e offr., Nigeria, 1908; served Cameroons 7. force, 1914-15; ment. in French desps. ecd. sp. appreciation of S. of S. for ser- rendered; admy. transport dept., 1916-17; led to Army as major, R.E., 1917; lieut.- E. and asst. dir., inland water transport, otamia, 1919; ment. in desps. and awarded . (mily. divn.): resumed duty, Nigeria, dep. dir., marine, 1925; capt. on retired 929; dir., 1930.

HEATON, GEORGE HERBERT. — B. 1896 ; ed. privately and Oxford Univ. ; lieut., 1/1st Kent egt. and R.F.C., 1915 ; H.M. Borstal instns., 24 ; supt., prisons, Uganda, 1927 ; comsnr., risons, Tanganyika Territory, 1931.

HEARST, SIR WILLIAM HOWARD, K.C.M.G. (17). — B. 1864 ; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll. t., and Toronto Univ. ; called to the bar, 1888 ; J., 1908 ; prominent legal practitioner at Sault l. Marie, Ontario ; now head of firm Hearst, wland and Atkin ; has been counsel in all more portant civil and criminal cases in Algoma trict in recent years ; unsuccessful cand. for is. assembly, Ontario, 1894 ; elec. at g.e. 1908, 1, and 1914 ; min. of lands, forests and mines Whitney administration, 1911 ; premier of tario, 1914 ; also min. of agric. ; defeated at ., Oct., 1919 ; resig. with his govt., Nov., 1919 ; m., internat. joint comsn. (Can. section), 23rd b., 1920.

HEARNE, HORACE HECTOR. — B. 1892 ; rister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn ; asst. dist. comsnr.,anda, Jan., 1916 ; 1st cls. mag., Aug., 1918 ; . mag., Jan., 1921 ; snr. mag., Nov., 1924 ; puisne judge on various occasions, 1925-26 and 8-30 ; ag. chan. of diocese, Nov., 1929 ; mem., M.'s ct. of appl. for Eastern Africa, 1930 ; chan. diocese, 1932 ; puisne judge, Tanganyika territory, 1933 ; ag. ch. just., 1935 and 1936 ; sne just., Ceylon, 1936.
IVES, HON. WILLIAM CARLOS, B.C.L. — B. 73; ed. pub. schl., Pincher Creek, Alta., oatcook, Queb., and McGill Univ.; puisne idge, sup. ct., Alta., 1914.

HEDDON, JOHN DONALD, B.A. (Oxon). — B. 879 ; clk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1903 ; prin. lk., East London, 1915 ; survr., Durban, 1916 ; h. clk., head office, Pretoria, 1918 ; dep. comsnr., ust. and excise, 1926 ; comsnr. cust. and excise, 1930 ; del. for Union govt. at Imp. Just. Confce., 1921 ; cust. adviser to delegn. to Australian govt. (flour shipments), 1921 ; mem., delegn. to Kenya to rept. on trade prospects, 1922 ; rep. of Union govt. at economic conference, Antananarivo, Madagascar, 1923 ; advisory mem., Imp. Confce. 1926 ; del. for Union govt., Imp. Confce., 1930 ; adviser, Union govt., Ottawa Econ. Confce., 1932. HEDLEY, HAROLD THOMAS, Incorporated Acct. — B. 1886 ; audit dept., S.S., Dec., 1921 ; i/c. external audit dept., K. Lumpur, Jan., 1922 ; asst. audr., grade A, Jan., 1923 ; ag. sr. asst. audr., Nov., 1928-Jly., 1929 ; audr., Penang, Aug., 1930 ; snr. asst. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., July, 1933 ; ag. dep. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., Aug., 1934.

SAACS, RT. HON. SIR ISAAC ALFRED, . (1921), G.C.M.G. (1932), K.C.M.G. (1928).— 1855; called to the bar, Victoria, 1880; Q.C., ; M.L.A., Victoria, 1892-1901; solr.-gen., 3; atty.-gen., 1894-9; and again in 1900; mem. Aust. Fed. Convention, 1897-8; and of first leral parlmt.; atty.-gen. in Deakin ministry, 5-6; judge of High Ct. of Australia, Oct., 6; ag. ch. just., 1925 and 1927; ch. just., r., 1930; gov. gen. of the Commonwealth of stralia, Jan., 1931.

flagged_value.position: judge of High court of Australia

source_text: IVEY, LIEUT. COMMANDER. A. V. P., R.D., .N.R. — B. 1894; Royal Navy, 1915; sub. eut., R.N.R., 1917; lieut. commdr., 1927; commdr. (ret. list), 1935; Cameroons expedy. orce, 1915-16; Harwich force destroyers, 1916-17; ommanded Torpedo boats, 1918; commanded I.M. river gunboat "Robin" on West River, hina, 1919; marine offr., Nigeria, 1920; harb. nast., Port Harcourt, 1926; prin. marine offr., 930; ag. dir., marine, Oct., 1934 to May, 1935; lir., marine, 1935.

NELSON, KT. (BACH.). B.A.—B. 1870; ed. at S. Africa Coll., and R. Schl. of Mines, Clausthal, Germany; gold med., S.A. Coll. and of Chem. Met. and Mining Soc. of S.A.; asst. engnr. and consulting engnr. to Transvaal Gold Fields Ltd., and subsidiary coys., 1896-1907; govt. mining engnr., Transvaal, 1st Jan., 1908; ditto, Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chancellor, Univ. of Witwatersrand; ret., 1st Nov., 1926.

LIEUT.-COL. SIR HERBERT, K.C.M.G. E., C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1846; lieut. 1866; employed under War Office at Sydney House in 1868 to 1870, and 1 of sunken ships, from submarine nd under G.P.O. in the telegraph m 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; the campaign of 1873-74 on the priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, 7, to Feb., 1878; sec. to the col. comtee., 1878; sec. to the roy. comsn. fence of Br. possessions and commerce 1879-82; specially employed to visit it on the defences of Singapore and 1883-4; sec. col. defence comtee., 1885; to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of Ireland, 5, to Jan., 1886; sec. to roy. comsn. turne centennial exhibn., 1888; priv. ord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-5; sec. to comsn. for the Paris exhibition, 1900.

flagged_value.position: priv. secretary to the Earl of Carnarvon

source_text: MINTER, K. S. — B. 1894; apptd. after npet. exam. asst. clk., and assigned to G.P.O., pt., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to M. stationery office, Aug., 1914; to C.O., st., 1914; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 22.

EALE, HAROLD BERNARD.—Second clk. to comsnr., Southern div., Bech. Prot., June, 1909; 2nd clk. to asst. comsnr., Northern Prot.; sed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1911; , 1912; ag. asst. res. mag., 1913; sub-inspr., h. Prot. pol., 1914; ag. res. mag., 1914; stated local rank of capt.; res. mag., 1921; sed 3rd grade Secwana exam., 1921.

RMSBY-GORE, RT. HON. WILLIAM GEORGE rhur, P.C. (1927).—B. 1885; ed. Eton t New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); late capt., opshire Yeomanry; intell. officer Arab eau, 1916-17; asst. sec., War Cabinet, 1917- asst. polit. offr., Palestine, 1918; British m., Permanent Mandates Comsn., L. of N., 1-22; M.P., Stafford since Jan., 1910; ompanied C.O. mission to British West lies, 1921-22; parly. under sec. of state Colonies, 1st Nov., 1922 to 23rd Jan., 4 and since Nov., 1924; chmn., parly. comsn. E. Africa, 1924; apptd. by S. of S. to visit and t. on W. African dependencies, Jan., 1926; of S. for the Cols., 1936.

RR, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM JAMES, K.C.M.G. 28), C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1870; ed. Bath Coll. i Woolwich Acad.; 1st comsn., Royal Artillery, Feb., 1889; capt., Sept., 1899; maj., 4; ret., Sept., 1908, served with Chitral ief force, 1895 (medal with clasp); Indian ntier, 1897 (clasp); Tirah expedit. force, 1897- (clasp); China war, 1900-01 (medal); S. rican war, 1900-02 (medal, 2 clasps); 3rd cls. ., N Nigeria, Feb., 1903; 2nd cls. res., 1904; . cls. res., 1908; ag. sec. to admnstr., N. Nigeria, y to Sept., 1907; ch. sec., Cyprus, Feb., 1911; mnstd. govt., July to Oct., 1911, and Apr. to ag., 1913; ret. on pension on account of ill th, Feb., 1917; attd. W.O., Int. branch, 1918- ; col. sec., Gibraltar, June, 1919; ag. gov., on veral occasions: gov. and commdr.-in-ch. of the hamas, 1926-1931; ret.

PRITCHETT, CECIL JOHN SUCKLING, B.A. (con.).—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civil serv., Dec., 2; attached to Jaffna Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; pol. mag., Trincomalee, July, 1913; ag. asst. ltmt. offr., Nov., 1913; attached to Jaffna chcheri, Aug., 1914. PROBYN, SIR LESLIE, K.C.M.G. (1909); M.G. (1903).—Ed. at Charterhouse, and in ance and Germany, called to the bar, d. Tem., 1884; joint author of "The risdiction and Practice of the Mayor's Court," i other law books; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, B; atty.-gen. of Grenada, May, 1896; also ag. .sec. in 1897-8; ag. admstr. on several occasions; . secretary, S. Nigeria, 12th Apr., 1901; gov., era Leone, 14th July, 1904; gov., Barbados, i Nov., 1910; assumed govt., 13th Feb., 1911.

PTOLEMY, WM. JOHN, I.S.O., J.P.—B. 1850; .. at Smithville gram. schl., Ontario; served ith G.W. Rly. Co. of Canada, 1873-1875; P.R. Dominion govt. telegraph construction, 75; mercantile life, 1876-1882; entd. govt. rv. as acctnt., treasury dept., Manitoba, 1883 to 385; municipal comsnr., 1886; dep. prov. ear., 1886 to date.
UTLEDGE, GEORGE ANTHONY LEY.—B. 3; ed. Sherborne schl. and Worcester Coll., Ord, B.A. 1935; Territorial Army (R. A.) 3-35; asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 1935.

PROUT, W. T., C.M.G. (1905), M.B., C.M. (din.).—Asst. poor law med. offr., Mauritius, 35; surg. supt., immigrant ship "Dundee," 37; asst. col. surg., Gold Coast, 1888; dist. nsnr., Gold Coast, 1890; col. surg., Gambia, 3; ag. chief mag., Gambia, 1893; med. r. in charge of base hosp. during campaign against Foday Sila; col. surg., S. Leone, 1895, title vered to prin. med. offr., 1901; retired, 1906; low of roy. med. soc., Edin., and corrpdg. sm. of the epidemiological soc. of Lond.; lect., il. of trop. med., Univ. of Liverpool; med. visor to C.O.; mem. of advisory, med. and litary comtee. for Tropical Africa, C.O.; thor of article "Yaws" in "Diseases of Warm mates" (Davidson); Fil. Voloulous (Archives Parasitologie, May, 1901); "Filariasis in S. one" (Brit. Med. Journal, 1902); and other pers.

SAINT, SYDNEY JOHN, B.Sc., A.I.C..—B. 1897; Beauminster Grammar Schl., Reading Univ.; Sc., 1920; served with R.A.F., 1916-19; Salters research fellow, 1920-22; lect. in agrl. chemistry, eds Univ., 1922-26; agrl. chemist, Barbados, 1927; dist. dir. and chem., dept., sci. and agr., 1936.

YRIE, MAJ.-GEN. HON. SIR GRANVILLE DE JNE, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (16), V.D.—B. 1865; ed. King's Schl., Ramatta; mem., state parl't., N.S.W., 3 years, n., house of reps., C. of A., 1911-27; asst. sec. for defence, 1919-22; commanded 3rd ht Horse Regt., N.S.W., for 7 yrs. with rank lieut.-col.; served S. Africa, 1900-01 (severely wounded (Queen's med. 4 clasps); European war, 1914-18; commanded 2nd Light Horse g., A.I.F., Egypt; commanded Australian n., Syria; G.O.C., Australian Forces, Egypt (wounded twice, five times ment. in desps.); maj.-gen. commdg. 1st Cav. Divn., N.S.W., 1923-27; high comsnr. for Australia, 1927-32.

SEAGOE, ERNEST GOLDFINCH, M.B.E.—B. 36; clk. to res. comsnr., New Hebrides, 1902; spr. of native labourers, Br. residency, New brides, 1909; ag. asst. to res. comsnr., 1911-12 d 1915-16; joint ag. native advocate, Oct., 15, to Dec., 1922; ag. asst. to res., Nov., 1919 Feb., 1920 and Sept. to Dec., 1921; commdt., nstab., Aug., 1922; dep. comsnr., W. Pacific ew Hebrides). Nov., 1922; in ch. of accts., ay to Aug., 1923; ag. asst. to res. comsnr.. various occasions, 1923-33; dist. agt. and lab. spr., Apr., 1923; agt., Efate dist. and commdt., l. (Br. divn.) Apr., 1925.

SEED, CHARLES WILLIAM STANLEY.—B. 1898; d. St. John's (R.C.) Coll., Wouersh, Surrey; artists' Rifles, 1916; 2nd lieut., 12th London Regt., 1917; served in France, 1918-19; (G.S. nd Victory Meds.); col. audit dept., July, 1921; asst. audr., Nigeria, Sept., 1921; ag. senr. asst. udr., July-Sept., 192; asst. audr., Nyasaland, Dec., 1926; ag. senr. asst. audr., June, 1927 to July, 1928 and Oct., 1928 to Sept., 1929; audr., Seychelles and Somaliland, July, 1930; also pvte. sec. to gov., Seychelles, 1931-33 and to O.A.G., July-Sept., 1933; senr. asst. audr., S.S. and F.M.S., 1936.

M.I.C.E.—B. 1857; asst. engnr. govt. rlys., Trinidad, 1874; dist. engnr., Couva extension rly., 1878; res. engnr. in charge of construction, San Fernando and Guaiacara rlys., 1879; 1st asst. engnr., P.W. dept., Jan., 1885; has acted as asst. dir. of P.W., 1885, 6, 8, 9, and 90; engnr., N. div., P.W.D., Jan., 1892; 1st engnr. in charge of rds. and bdges., P.W.D., Jan., 1894; engnr. in charge of rds. and bdges, rds. and bdges. dept., Jan., 1895; asst. dir. of pub. wks., and sen. div. engnr., Jan., 1897; has acted as D.P.W. with seat in legis. coun., 1894, 5, 8, 9, 1900, 2, 5, 6, and 7.

TROWBRIDGE, EDMUND.—B. 1881; ed. pub del and high schls., Toronto, Ontario; asst. ., exec. coun., 1906; sec., Alberta coal comsn., 07; sec., Alberta pork comsn., 1908; dep. prov. . and registr. of companies, prov. of Alberta, 1910.

THOMSON, FRANK DAVID, B.A., C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1877; ed. High Schl., Napier and Canterbury and Victoria Colls., N.Z.; cadet. educn. dept., 1894; priv. sec. to various ministers since 1901; sec. to prime min., since 1912; clk. of exec. coun. and sec. of cabinet since 1919; permt. head, prime minister's dept., 1926; mem. of secretariat, Br. Empire delegation, Peace Confce., Paris, 1919; mem., Imp. Confce. secretariat, 1917, 1918, 1921, 1923 and 1926. THOMSON, SIR GRAEME, K.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.B.—B. 1875; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; higher div. clk., Admlty., 1900; asst. dir. of trans., 1914; dir. of trans., 1914; col. sec., Ceylon, Sept., 1919; offr. admstg. govt., 8th-18th Jan., 1920 and Mar.-Sept., 1920; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, B. Guiana, 1923; gov. and commander-in-chief, Nigeria, 1925.

SINCKLER, EDWARD GOULBURN.—B. 1856; entered col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Nov., 1874; acted as clk. to lieut.-gov., 1878; 2nd clk. corrspndce. branch, col. sec.'s office, 1879; ag. asst. clk. to Gov. W. Robinson, 1880; 1st clk., record branch, col. sec.'s office, and clk., courts of ordinary and error, Jan., 1883; ag. ch. clk., 1886; comsnr. of census, 1891; ch. clk. to judges, asst. ct. of appeal, 1892; J.P., Oct., 1892; ag. registr., friendly socs., June, 1893; ag. scn. pol. mag., Bridgetown, and dist. A, Aug. to Oct., 1893; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1894; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. F, July, 1896, to Jan., 1897; ag. pol. mag., judge, and coroner, dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1897; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. D, Mar. to Apl., 1898; ag. coroner, St. Michael, 1898 (at time of hurricane), and has repeatedly acted as such; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, 1899; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, Feb., 1900; ag. comsnr of probate, May to Nov., 1900; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, July, 1900, to Sept., 1901; pol. mag. and judge, dist. E, Oct., 1901; discharged duties of pol. mag. and judge, dist. D, in addition to own duties, Sept., 1905; and from Aug. to Oct., 1907; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, 18th July to 23rd Nov., 1906; coroner, dist. E, 27th May, 1907; ag. comsnr. of probates, May to Aug., 1903, and May to Oct., 1908; ag. judge, petty debt. ct., Bridgetown, July to Oct., 1909; ag. pol. mag. and judge, Dist. "D," in addition to own duties, Aug. and Oct., 1910, and May to Aug., 1911; comsnr. of census, 1911; mem. and hon. sec. of historic sites comtee., 1910; suggested Nelson centenary postage stamp, 1904; took active part in promotion of celebration of tercentenary of Barbados, 1905; compiler of first "Barbados Government Handbook," 1912, and compiler of handbooks for 1913 and 1914; man. of Barbados govt. savings bk., Aug., 1919.

REMLETT, FREDERICK THOMAS GEORGE, .E. (1918).—B. 1881; ed. Denstone Coll., Fs. ; 2nd lieut., Jamaica Militia, 1902; lieut., 3; attached Lancs. Fusiliers, 1903; sub.-inspr. stab., Jamaica, 1903; ag. inspr., ditto, 1908- 1; studied finger-print system, Scotland Yard, 3; J.P., 1909; inspr. of constab., Jamaica, 2; supt., Spanish Town fire brigade, 1911-1912; sed mil. schl. of instn., Chelsea, 1912; transfd. Mauritius pol., 1912; dep. inspr.-gen. of pol., uritius, 1916; capt. and adjt., Mauritius volr. ce, 1916; capt. in army, 1917; raised and comd. uritius labour batt., 1917; comd. Mauritius . force. Mar. to Dec., 1919; ag. inspr.-gen. of ., Mauritius, in 1917 and 1919; major, 1920; inspr.-gen., supt., prisons and O.C., Mauritius l. Force, Feb. to Sept., 1922; dep. comsnr., . and prisons, Uganda, Nov., 1922; ag. nsnr., pol. and prisons, on many occasions, 2-30; comsnr., pol. and prisons, Uganda, c., 1930.

WEBB, FREDERICK WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1837; sess. clk., legis. coun., N.S. Wales, 1851; clk., G.P.O., 1853; clk., legis. assem. dept., 1860; ag. clk. of assem., 1877-86; clk., 1888; apptd. J.P. of col., 1888; sec. to National Australasian Convention, 1891.

WERRY, CAPT. FRANCIS EDWARD.—Ed. at I. of Wight Coll. and Uppingham schl.; capt., 4th batt. Essex regt.; dist. comsnr., Lagos, July, 1902; ag. comsnr. of pol., Aug. to Oct., 1902; ag. trav. comsnr., N.E. dist., Jan. to June, 1903.
COWIE, D.D.—Was chaplain with Lord Clyde's army at the capture of Lucknow; chaplain to Viceroy of India, 1863; apptd. rector of Stafford, 1867; primate of N.Z., 1869.

FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1847; clk., dept. of just., Canada, 1869; transf. to dept. of S. of S. as clk.-in-charge of mounted pol. branch, Apr., 1876; ch. clk., Sept., 1878; controller of N.-W. mounted pol. force of Canada, Nov., 1878; dep. head, July, 1883; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir John Macdonald, 1880 to 1882; comsnr. of N. W. Territories, 24th Aug., 1905.
BERNARD.—Serv 99th regt., 183 J.P. for col. (32), M.A., called to sup. ct. c. M. Apr. to Oct., 1895; dist. cashier, Flacq, June, 1898.

GIBSON, JAS. YOUNG.—Clerk and Zulu interp. Ct. of R.M.; sub. distrib. of stps., and regr. circuit ct., Newcastle, Natal, April, 1882; also p. clerk of peace, Mar., 1887; asst. comsr. and M., Nqutu dist., Zululand, Feb., 1889; ditto, twandwo dist., May, 1889.

flagged_value.position: sub. distrib. of stps., and registrar circuit court

source_text: GILKARD, M. A.—Began his political life with the organization of Manitoba as a province of the Dominion; was a member of the local government under the first three lieutenant-governors, and occupied the positions of provincial treasurer, provincial secretary, and minister of agriculture; in 1872 appointed senior member of the north west council and a senator of Canada, which latter position he still holds. GIROUARD, DESIRÉ, Q.C.—Born 1836. Ed. Montreal Coll., called to the bar, Canada, 1860; B.L. 1876; represented Jacques Cartier County, 1878 to 1895; judge supreme ct., Canada, 1895.

flagged_value.position: senior member of the north west council

source_text: ROBERT MACHRAY, D.D. — Late dean and fellow of Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge, and vicar of Madingley; graduated in 1855, when he was 34th Wrangler. The diocese of Rupert's Land is 37,000 square miles in extent, and has a population of 200,000 souls.

TRIMEN, ROLAND, F.R.S., F.L.S., F.Z.S.—Curator of South African Museum, Cape Town, Cape of Good Hope; 3rd class clerk, auditor-general's office, Cape Town, July, 1860; transferred to colonial secretary's office, March, 1862; 2nd class clerk, July, 1865; acting curator of museum, March, 1866; accompanied governor Sir H. Barkly to Transvaal West, as acting private secretary, Aug., 1872; 1st class clerk, Nov., 1872; curator museum, in conjunction with duties in colonial secretary's office, Jan., 1873; acting despatch and confidential clerk to governor, Aug., 1873; acting chief clerk in office of commissioner of crown lands and public works, Jan., 1875, and Jan., 1876; secretary to local committee for the Philadelphia Centennial Exhibition of 1876, July, 1875; secretary to premier, the Hon. J. C. Molteno, on his special mission to England in connection with South African affairs, July, 1876; curator of South African Museum, Aug., 1876; delegate to the International Phylloxera Congress at Bordeaux, Oct., 1881; presdt., Phylloxera Commn., Jan., 1886. Is author of "Khopolacera Africo Australis; a descriptive catalogue of South African butterflies, &c.," and of various papers on ornithology, entomology, and botany in the transactions of the Linnaean, Zoological, and Entomological Societies, the Quarterly Journal of Science, and other periodicals.

flagged_value.position: acting despatch and confidential clerk to governor
acting chief clerk in office of commissioner of crown lands and public works
acting chief clerk in office of commissioner of crown lands and public works
secretary to local committee for the Philadelphia Centennial Exhibition of 1876
secretary to premier, the honorary J. C. Molteno, on his special mission to England in connection with South African affairs
delegate to the International Phylloxera Congress at Bordeaux

source_text: GREEN, JAMES ERNEST.—B. 1860; ed. at Cheltenham Coll., and Trin. Hall, Camb., 1879; B.A., LL.B., Law Tripos, 1882; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Mar., 1884; admitted a solr., Mar., 1895; restored to the bar, 1905; solr.-gen. (to act as atty.-gen.), S. Nigeria Prot., May, 1905; solr.-gen. of Colony and Prot. of S. Nigeria, May, 1906; ag. chief just. of Prot., 1907; ag. puisne judge of Prot., 1907, and of Colony and Prot., 1908; ag. atty.-gen. of Colony and Prot., 1907 and 1908; asstd. to edit Laws of S. Nigeria, 1908.

RGYLE, SIR STANLEY SEYMOUR, K.B.E. (30), M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Ed.)—B. 1867; ed. Trinity Coll., Melbourne Univ.; lieut.-col., A.A.M.C., A.I.F. in European war; M.L.A., Victoria since 1920; ch. sec. four ministries, 1923-29; premier and treas. of Victoria, May, 1932-Apr., 1935; leader of opposition since Apr., 1935.

AMERASINGHE, SAMSON FELIX, B.A., antab.—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., July, 26; attd., Kalutara kach., Aug., 1926; office st. govt. agt., E. Prov., Dec., 1927; pol. mag. andagamuwa, Sept., 1928; ditto, Negombo, Dec., 28; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, June, 30; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., June, 32; pol. mag., Kandy, Nov., 1932; addnl. asst. vt. agt., Colombo, Oct., 1935.

ABNER, CHARLES J.—B. 1876; on mily. , France and Flanders, Sept., 1914 to Mar., 9; clk., Army pay office, R. Engurs., Mar. to ., 1919; clk., Army pay office, command mr., Overseas Bases, Oct., 1919 to July, 1922; offr., C.O., 10th July, 1922.

AMBLER, LLOYD EDWARD.—B. 1894; clk., stal dept., Fiji, June, 1909; 4th cls. clk., ids dept., Aug., 1913; served with 1st Batt., R.R.C., France, 1916-17; Indian Army Res. comsnd., Oct., 1917; Baluchistan, 1917-19; 1 cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1916; td., lands dept., Fiji, Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd clk. and sec., mining bd., Oct., 1919-Oct., 20; ag. sub-inspr. constab. (during Indian ike), Feb., 1920; ag. 1st cls. clk. and clk., exec. d leg. couns., Jan. to Apr., 1921; regy. clk., estern Pacific high comsn., May, 1921; ag. ch. r., Sept., 1922 to Feb., 1923; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 27; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., on various occa-sns., 1923-31; ag. sec., Western Pacific high msn., June to Aug., 1931; ch. clk. and acct., n., 1932.

UZEN, ALLAN LECKIE.—Served in R.E., 1-2; S. African War (medal and five clasps); se police, 1902-6; Bech. Prot. police, 1906-14; sed Cape civ. serv. lower law exam., 1914; clk. to asst. comsnr., N. Dist., Bech. Prot., 4; ag. asst. res. mag., ditto, 1915; passed 2nd le Secwana exam., 1916; 1st grade (B), ditto, 7; ag. res. mag., 1917; ag. asst. res. mag., 9; res. mag., 1921.

(1932), P., F.R.G.S.—B. 1887; ed. Borough Road , Isleworth, London, 1906-08; teacher's certif. h 4 distinctions) Bd. of Educn., Lond.; A.C.T. , Bd. of Educn., Lond.; staff-capt., H.M. ces; inspr. Q.M.G. services; maths. and draw- master, Junr. Tech. Schl., Liverpool, 1919; prin., t. training coll., Trinidad, Jan., 1921; mem., er-Col. Educn. Confce, 1921; mem. bd. ist training; supt., tech. classes, 1922; ag. r. inspr. schls., Feb., 1923; senr. inspr. schls., nidad, 1st July, 1923; ag. dir., educn., July, 5 to Mar., 1926; asst. dir., educn. and senr. or., schls., June, 1926; asst. dir., educn. and inspr., schls., Jan., 1928; ag. dir., educn., v to Nov., 1929; dir., educn., 1934; author est Indian Readers " etc.

KNIGHT, WALTER AUGUSTUS. — Copyist, G.P.O., Grenada, May, 1903; 2nd clk., G.P.O., June, 1904; clk., supt. of wks., Jan., 1906; clk., pol. mag., S. Dist., May, 1907; rev. offr. and road survr., W. Dist., Jan., 1909; addtnl. coroner, St. Mark's parish, Jan. 1913; road survr., N. Dist., Dominica, Dec., 1914; comsnr., Carrisac, Dec., 1935.

PARSONS, T. R., M.B.E.—b. 1898; mil. serv., 1916–19; boy clk., G.P.O., 1914; asst. clk. (clerical offr.), 1916; trans. to C.O., 1923; higher clerical offr., 1938; higher exec. offr., 1944; senr. exec. offr., 1948; chief exec. offr., 1954.

SUTHERLAND, A. R. — M.A., Aberdeen Univ.; Science mast., Glen Urquhart H.G. schl., 1903-5; asst. mast., Campbelltown gram. schl., 1905-6; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong. 1906; lecturer in biology, Hong Kong Coll. of med., 1909-1910; ag. asst. master, pol. schl., 1909 and 1912.

THORNTON, SWINFORD LESLIE, B.A.—Lincoln Coll., Oxon.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1877; admitted to the bar Straits Settlements, Nov., 1886; registrar, supreme court; commissioner, court of requests, and collector of stamps, Malacca, June, 1886; actg. sen. mag., Singapore, July, 1888; regr., sup. ct., and mag., Malacca, July, 1892; atty. gen., St. Vincent, 1894; ag. admr., Nov., 1894, to Mar., 1895; ag. ch. jt., Nov., 1895; resdt. mag., Jamaica, 1896.

flagged_value.position: commissioner, court of requests, and collector of stamps

source_text: LEONG PENG CHONG.—b. 1911 ; ed. Anglo-Chinese Sch. and Raffles Coll., S'pore and Camb. Univ. ; asst. to prof. of biochemistry, King Edward VII Coll. of Medicine, S'pore, 1931 ; tutor in biochemistry, 1938 ; lectr., biochemistry, Univ. of Mal., 1949 ; senr. lectr., chemistry, 1950 ; biochemist, gen. hosp., S'pore, 1953.

ARKE, CAPT. L. S.—Comsn., 7th Batt., npshire Regt., 1909 ; Coldstream Guards, 1 ; on war ser., Uganda and India ; 1913-18 ; cted by W.O. for serv. with Yemen Infy., on, 1919-20 ; cadet, Zanzibar pol., 1923-25 ; fin. advr. and comdt., pol., govt. of Bah., Persian Gulf, 1926-32 (twice acted as advr. govt.) ; 1st grade inspr., Mauritius pol., 1933.
